title: Surah Ar-Rahman Decoded: The Question God Asks 31 Times

sound.ambient: calm paradise garden ambience, soft flowing water, gentle airy breeze, distant soft birdsong, serene and clean, no music, no melody, no instruments

_note: Surah Decoded #4 - Ar-Rahman. Generic data-driven Decoded kit + emerald gardens palette (EmeraldVeil + heavens backdrops) for per-surah visual variety. Calm tone. Verses 55:1-4, 55:13 (the 31x refrain), 55:26-27 auto-pulled. QURAN-ONLY by design: Ar-Rahman's famous fada'il hadith are contested in grading, so to stay bulletproof we quote only the Quran. No em dashes.

segments.text: There is one verse in the Quran that God repeats thirty-one times. It is a question, asked again and again, to you. Tonight, we decode the surah it lives in.
segments.caption: Ar-Rahman, decoded.

segments.text: Surah Ar-Rahman. The Most Merciful. The fifty-fifth chapter of the Quran.
Seventy-eight verses. One single theme, mercy. And one verse that returns, thirty-one times.
It opens without even a name. Just this. The Most Merciful. Taught the Quran. Created man. And taught him to speak.

data.heading: How mercy unfolds
rows: He taught the Quran, created man
Sun and moon by precise calculation
He set the Balance, do not cheat it
Man from clay, jinn from fire
Two seas meet, yet never mix
All perishes but the Face of your Lord

segments.text: From the gift of speech, to the sun and moon, to the scale of justice, to two gardens waiting in Paradise. Watch the mercy unfold.
And between every gift, the same question returns. So which of the favors of your Lord will you deny? You will hear it thirty-one times.

data.question: That one verse repeats how many times in the surah?

segments.text: So how many times does that single verse repeat? Can you guess? Comment it.
And then the reminder that puts it all in place. Everyone on the earth will perish. And there remains only the Face of your Lord, full of majesty and honor.

data.heading: What it teaches
items: Mercy is the lens of the whole surah.
His creation runs on perfect balance.
Be just; never tip the scale.
All things fade, but His Face remains.

segments.text: Mercy is the lens. His creation runs on balance. So be just, and never tip the scale. Because all things fade, but His Face remains.
Mercy taught you to speak. Mercy gave you the scale. So which of His favors will you deny? Read Surah Ar-Rahman.
segments.caption: Read Ar-Rahman.

segments.text: And comment the surah you want me to decode next.
segments.caption: Which one next?

segments.text: And our app, Ketabi, is a calm home for your faith. Get it free, at Ketabi Studio dot com.
segments.caption: Download Ketabi.

Quran 55:1-4 (the Most Merciful taught the Quran, created man, taught him speech) - auto-pulled
